input.prompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a .NET MAUI issue with keyboard handling in .NET 10. This is a known change — in .NET 9+, `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` was deprecated/removed because Android itself deprecated `adjustResize`. The replacement in .NET MAUI is to use the new `KeyboardAccelerator` or more specifically, the `IKeyboardAutoManagerScroll` feature.

Let me think about this more carefully:

In .NET MAUI (.NET 8+), there's a `KeyboardAutoManagerScroll` that automatically scrolls content when the keyboard appears. In Android, `adjustResize` was deprecated in Android 11 (API 30). 


For .NET 9+, MAUI switched to using `KeyboardAutoManagerScroll` by default, which handles keyboard adjustments through the modern `WindowInsetsCompat` and edge-to-edge APIs on Android instead of the old `adjustResize` approach. For chat-style layouts with a bottom input field, this should automatically scroll the content up when the keyboard appears, though there might be edge cases that need custom handling. Setting up the inset listener in MainActivity... I'm setting up the Android implementation to handle keyboard visibility by listening to window insets and adjusting the chat layout's bottom padding dynamically, which keeps the input field visible without pushing content off-screen.
